author: @CyprienCole
text: BluSky feels a lot like Reddit: they're always hunting for excuses to ban people. When a platform seems more interested in banning users than keeping them, there's no good reason to stick around. I don't want to constantly dance around arbitrary rules that don't even matter. If they were just normal, common-sense guidelines, hardly anyone would get banned unless they were genuinely breaking the law.

Like when they wanted to ban The_Donald, they couldn't find a legitimate reason, so they created their quarantine system to suppress it.
And they had woke activists pretend to be Trump supporters posting things to get The_Donald banned. When they did finally ban it, they only did so by inventing new rules, which they only ever applied in one direction.

author: @johnx79486
text:
When the Left has control of a platform, they censor everyone to make the appearance that they are super majority opinion. They did this effectiely for sbout 10 maybe 15 years with the help of all the institutions. In fact, it's a testament to American culture that we endured cultural subversion, which has toppled countries all over the world. 

X, on the other hand, is now a fairly good representative of society because censorship is relatively lax on the platform. It skews right/moderate, but there are plenty of leftists and liberals on X, and it makes a more fun experience if you want to shitpost and antagonize each other.
